title: Problem with using ice40 bram
body:
The below module can be synthesized using 
yosys -p synth_ice40 -f verilog ram1.vl

```
module ram1 (input clk,
             input             sel,
             input             we, 
             input [SIZE-1:0]  adr, 
             input [63:0]      dat_i, 
             output reg [63:0] dat_o);
   parameter  SIZE = 5; // Address size

   reg [63:0] mem [0:(1 << SIZE)-1];
   integer    i;

   initial begin
      for (i = 0; i < (1<<SIZE) - 1; i = i + 1)
        mem[i] <= 0;
   end
   
   always @(posedge clk)
     if (sel & ~we)
       dat_o <= mem[adr];

   always @(posedge clk)
     if (sel & we)
       mem[adr] <= dat_i;
endmodule
```

Output is 

```
=== ram1 ===

   Number of wires:                  9
   Number of wire bits:            202
   Number of public wires:           6
   Number of public wire bits:     136
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                  7
     SB_LUT4                         3
     SB_RAM40_4K                     4
```

so we get to use a SB_RAM40_4K, as intended.

However, when I try merging the two always blocks, I get the following module, which as far as I
understand these things should be equivalent: 

```
module ram2 (input clk,
             input             sel,
             input             we, 
             input [SIZE-1:0]  adr, 
             input [63:0]      dat_i, 
             output reg [63:0] dat_o);
   parameter  SIZE = 5; // Address size

   reg [63:0] mem [0:(1 << SIZE)-1];
   integer    i;

   initial begin
      for (i = 0; i < (1<<SIZE) - 1; i = i + 1)
        mem[i] <= 0;
   end
   
   always @(posedge clk)
     if (sel) begin
       if (~we)
         dat_o <= mem[adr];
       else
         mem[adr] <= dat_i;
     end
endmodule
```

synthesis using yosys -p synth_ice40 -f verilog ram2.vl gives

```
=== ram2 ===

   Number of wires:               1684
   Number of wire bits:           3893
   Number of public wires:          38
   Number of public wire bits:    2184
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:               3821
     SB_DFFE                      2112
     SB_LUT4                      1709
```

So the ram isn't synthesized using SB_RAM40_4K, and synthesis is also a lot slower due to the much larger net.

In the logs, the first difference which looks interesting to me is for the MEMORY_DFF pass, with

    Checking cell `$memwr$\mem$ram1.vl:23$104' in module `\ram1': merged $dff to cell.
    Checking cell `$memrd$\mem$ram1.vl:19$67' in module `\ram1': merged data $dff with rd enable to cell.

for ram1.vl, and

    Checking cell `$memwr$\mem$ram2.vl:22$101' in module `\ram2': merged $dff to cell.
    Checking cell `$memrd$\mem$ram2.vl:20$69' in module `\ram2': no (compatible) $dff found.

for ram2.vl. If I read that correctly, it means yosys can't recognize the RAM read port in ram2.vl.

I would expect yosys to treat both versions identically, and generate the proper enable signals (sel & we for write, sel & ~we for read) no matter if it's expressed in one or two always block.

And nevertheless, yosys really is an awesome tool!

Best regards,
/Niels

PS. Apologies if formatting looks crappy, this is the first time ever I file an issue using github, and 
me and web forms usually have some difficulty to get on friendly terms.
